rulings.Case Title: JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LISA LEEBOVE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 27, 2010, line 7. PLAINTIFF JP BUILDERS INC'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is denied. rulings.Case Title: JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LISA LEEBOVE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 27, 2010, line 7. CROSS DEFENDANT JP BUILDERS, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled based on lack of jurisdiction. The 2nd cross-complaint is properly characterized as an amended cross-complaint that superseded the original cross-complaint. The subsequent dismissal of the original cross-complaint was a nullity. Overruled as to 1st and 2nd causes of action. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 3rd and 4th causes of action. Leebove to plead terms of the implied contract with more particularity. The court notes that Leebove relies on the express contract to argue that the lien is excessive. Overruled as to 5th cause of action for unjust enrichment. Sustained without leave to amend as to the 6th cause of action. Civil Code section 1584.5 cause of action fails as a matter of law because the parites agree that they were subject to some sort of contractual relationship. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LISA LEEBOVE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 27, 2010, line 7. CROSS DEFENDANT JP BUILDERS, INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is granted as to paragraph 59 without leave to amend. Grant with leave to amend as to paragraph 1 of the prayer to comply with C.C.P. section 425.10. Grant without leave to amend to pray for statutory damages because 6th cause of action fails. Grant without leave to amend as to paragraph 6 of the prayer because request is unnecessary. Deny as to remainder. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL OHAYON VS. CER, AN ENTITY OF UNKNOWN FORM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft'S Responses To Pltf'S First Set Of Discovery Requests And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 27, 2010, line 2. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL OHAYON'S Motion To Compel Defendant's Responses To Plaintiff's First Set Of Discovery Requests And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions is denied. Plaintiff should re-serve discovery requests, with proof of service, and defendants shall be given the statutory time to respond (30 days under C.C.P. section 2030.60). Request for sanctions denied.

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS NEFF VS. POPE & POPE PROPERTIES, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 27, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANTS POPE & POPE PROPERTIES, L.L.C., JAMES POPE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff must demonstrate that the action is outside the restraints of C.C.P. section 726 by pleading that the deed of trust is a nullity and facts showing how/why the deed is a nullity. Plaintiff must plead facts showing the representations were false to support the fraud cause of action. Demurrer would be overruled as to usury which may be raised as an affirmative defense. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK W. GRADY VS. NISSIM LANYADOO et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 27, 2010, line 6. Defendant Nissim Lanyadoo's Demurrer To Complaint is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MORGANS HOTEL GROUP CO., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al VS. TARSADIA HOTELS, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 27, 2010, line 5. DEFENDANTS TARSADIA HOTELS, HASINA, L.L.C., KALPANA, L.L.C., RIGG HOTEL, L.L.C., JRIA, L.L.C., TARSTONE HOTELS, L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 1st and 4th causes of action (breach of contract and unfair competition). Consideration for an agreement is not adequate when it is a mere promise to perform that which the promisor is legally bound to do. Plaintiff must allege a distinction between Morgans on Clift Holdings with respect to the parties' negotiations and activities relating to the Ground Lease. To support the unfair competition claim, plaintiff must further specify conduct that is unlawful, unfair, or fraudulent. As referenced in paragraph 43, plaintiff must allege facts regarding "discouraging potential buyers..." Demurrer overruled as to 2nd and 3rd causes of action. =(302/CWW)
